import mteb
bs = mteb.get_benchmarks()

        print(repr(b.name))
" 2>&1 | head -20

content.text:
Done. Wrote `GritLM/GritLM-7B` to `/app/result.txt`.

**How I determined it:** The `mteb` package and its results repo were available locally. I checked out the results repo at its end-of-August-2025 state (commit `71f6b62`, 2025-08-29) and used mteb's own aggregation to build the `MTEB(Scandinavian, v1)` leaderboard (28 tasks). Filtering to only models with results for **all 28 tasks** left 8 models, ranked by Mean (Task):

| Mean (Task) | Model |
|---|---|
| **64.08** | **GritLM/GritLM-7B** |
| 62.01 | intfloat/e5-mistral-7b-instruct |
| 61.62 | intfloat/multilingual-e5-large-instruct |
| 51.91 | NbAiLab/nb-sbert-base |
| … | … |

GritLM/GritLM-7B is the clear top, with a comfortable ~2-point margin, so the ranking is robust to any minor aggregation differences. Scratch files cleaned up and the results repo restored to its original branch.
